title: Error thrown when starting a container on podman 5.5.0-dev build on Windows
body:
### Issue Description

During overnight e2e tests on Podman Desktop the podman 5.5.0-dev version was used last night on Windows WSL overnight tests and all test suites failed in the same way with podman error being thrown whenever trying to start a container.

Error message is : `Error: (HTTP code 500) server error - netavark (exit code 1): iptables: No such file or directory (os error 2)`

Test run 1: https://github.com/podman-desktop/e2e/actions/runs/14585427741
Test run 2: https://github.com/podman-desktop/e2e/actions/runs/14585966700

I don't know if you want to investigate the test runs from above, but if you are looking for the logs you can click either link and go to the bottom of the page and download the win10 or win11 archives (since they have the same errors) and have traces/videos/console logs there.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. Have podman 5.5.0-dev installed.
2. Pull image `ghcr.io/linuxcontainers/alpine:latest`
3. Try to start a container with the image from step 2.
4. Notice the error being thrown.

In the e2e tests we have we do these steps from Podman Desktop, but it's not clear to me if that is a required step, my assumption is that it isn't.


### Describe the results you received

Describe the results you received

### Describe the results you expected

Describe the results you expected

### podman info output

```yaml
If you are unable to run podman info for any reason, please provide the podman version, operating system and its version and the architecture you are running.
```

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

None

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional information

Additional information like issue happens only occasionally or issue happens with a particular architecture or on a particular setting

comments.body:
Try starting the `iptables` service inside the Podman VM:
`podman machine ssh podman-machine-default "sudo systemctl start iptables"`

I am running (a freshly installed) podman 5.4.2 and I see the same issue.
I tried the wsl images: `quay.io/podman/machine-os-wsl:5.4` and `quay.io/podman/machine-os-wsl:5.5`, both giving the same error.
EDIT: I just saw 5.4 and 5.5 both point to the same image, so nvm this part.

Running the command suggested by @palbock yields: `Failed to start iptables.service: Unit iptables.service not found.`

iptables isn't even installed in these images:
```
$ podman machine ssh "dnf list --installed | grep iptables"
iptables-libs.x86_64                          1.8.10-15.fc41              fedora
$
```
Running a simple `podman machine ssh "dnf install iptables-legacy"` and then restarting fixed the issue

Yes don't use iptables.service it has nothing to do with podman. The issue is that no package seems to depend on iptables anymore which is expected for podman as we default to nftables for proper fedora, but because the WSL kernel is broken we cannot use proper nftables on windows https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/25201.

The fix is simple, install iptables which of course should be part of the image by default, I believe @l0rd is already working on that fix.
The WSL machine image is [getting rebuilt right now](https://cirrus-ci.com/build/4994813268852736). The problem should be fixed right after completion.
The new image has been build/pushed. I have been able to verify the fix locally. @cbr7, please confirm that the problem has been resolved on your side too.
@l0rd yeah, looks to be working now.
Can confirm the same testing with gbraad-actions/install-podman-action@dev on a windows runner
